text_description: Visible answer. For customer-side automation protections, explicitly name a written confidence-threshold SLA, auditable case-level error reporting with access to underlying records rather than aggregate-only dashboards, the customer's unilateral human-reversion right without vendor permission, and every evidenced monetary remedy as a directionally complete obligation: vendor owes the stated remedy to the customer for the exact evidenced failure class. Never omit an evidenced monetary remedy to save words.
